title: CodexGoalIterationReport
description: Codex's self-report for one iteration of the verifier-led goal loop. NOTE: self_assessed_status is advisory only — the loop's authoritative decision is made by declarative verifier commands (helper-run) plus Claude's review. The dispatcher (not Codex) executes the iteration's git commit using intended_commit_message + files_changed, so the workflow works on any mount/sandbox where Codex's seatbelt profile may block .git/ writes (e.g., /Volumes/* mounts with com.apple.provenance xattrs on macOS). DISPATCHER-INJECTED FIELDS (post-validation, NOT in the schema because codex exec --output-schema runs in strict structured-output mode which forbids optional properties under additionalProperties:false): after this schema validates codex's response, the dispatcher injects four additional fields into the result file before returning it to the caller: `committed` (boolean — true if git commit succeeded, false otherwise), `commit_sha` (string|null — short SHA of the commit, null if none), `unclaimed_changes` (array of strings — dirty paths observed post-commit that codex did not declare in files_changed), and `ignored_changes` (array of strings — gitignored paths that are new or content-changed since the pre-codex baseline). Callers reading the result file see all four; codex itself never sees or sets them.

summary.description: One-paragraph summary of what was done this iteration.

self_assessed_status.description: Codex's self-assessment. 'complete' = Codex believes verifiable_end_state is met. 'in_progress' = work done, more iters expected. 'blocked' = cannot proceed without external input. Authoritative status comes from verifiers, not this field.

blocker.description: If status != 'complete': what is preventing further progress this iteration. null when status='complete'.

files_changed.description: Paths of files you modified this iteration (relative to repo root). The dispatcher uses this list to `git add` your work, so be accurate — only files in this list will be staged and committed.

intended_commit_message.description: Conventional-commit message you want the dispatcher to use for this iteration's commit. The dispatcher runs `git add <files_changed>` + `git commit -m "<intended_commit_message>"` on your behalf, OUTSIDE your sandbox. DO NOT run git commit yourself — your sandbox may not have write access to .git/ on protected mounts. Set to null when no files were changed this iteration (no commit needed).
